senses.definition: a large organization that serves a religious, educational, social, or similar function

examples.x: The university is a well-respected institution known for its research programs.
The charity functions as an institution dedicated to helping the homeless.
The church is considered an important institution in the community.
The museum has become a cultural institution in the city.

senses.definition: an elementary school attended by children between the ages of 6 and 12

examples.x: Grade school students often participate in various activities like art, music, and physical education.
The teacher's dedication to her grade school students helped them excel academically and socially.
Parents play a crucial role in supporting their children's education during grade school years.
The curriculum in grade school focuses on building foundational skills in math, reading, and writing.

senses.definition: a department in a university or college that offers graduates an advanced or further degree

examples.x: After completing her undergraduate studies, she decided to apply to graduate school.
He was accepted into the graduate school of engineering at a prestigious university.
Graduate school programs typically focus on specialized fields of study.

senses.definition: a school for students between an elementary school and a high school, typically those in the 7th and 8th grades

examples.x: Junior high school students often experience significant social and academic transitions compared to elementary school.
She made new friends and explored various subjects during her time in junior high school.
The junior high school curriculum includes core subjects like math, science, language arts, and social studies.
Teachers at junior high school level aim to prepare students for the challenges of high school and beyond.
Transitioning from elementary school to junior high school involves adapting to new schedules, classrooms, and responsibilities.

senses.definition: a school attended by students between the ages of 14 and 18

examples.x: Senior high school students often undertake advanced coursework and specialized subjects to prepare for college or vocational training.
The senior high school curriculum includes a variety of subjects, ranging from core academics like math and English to elective courses in arts, sciences, and technology.
Senior high school provides opportunities for students to participate in extracurricular activities, sports, and community service, fostering personal growth and leadership skills.
Graduating from senior high school is a significant achievement, marking the completion of secondary education and the transition to adulthood.

senses.definition: a course of study that is held during the summer vacations at a school, college, or university
a formal dance or gathering of high school students, typically held at the end of the senior year

examples.x: The students spent weeks preparing for their senior prom.
She bought a beautiful dress for her high school prom.
The school decorated the gymnasium for the prom with lights and balloons.
For many, prom is one of the most memorable events of high school.
He asked his best friend to be his date to the prom.

senses.definition: to officially register oneself or someone else as a participant in a course, school, etc.

examples.x: Students need to enroll in the classes for the upcoming semester.
Parents can enroll their children in after-school activities for skill development.
New employees are required to enroll in the company's orientation program.
She decided to enroll in a cooking class.

senses.definition: to enter one's name in a list of an institute, school, etc.

examples.x: She decided to register for the online course to improve her skills.
You need to register at the university’s office before attending classes.
He failed to register in time for the new semester and had to wait until next year.
She plans to register for the science club at the start of the school year.

senses.definition: a small closet that usually has a lock, in which valuable items and belongings could be stored

examples.x: She stored her books and backpack in her locker before heading to class.
He forgot the combination to his locker and had to ask the teacher for assistance.
Students are encouraged to keep their lockers organized and free from clutter.
The office provides lockers for employees to store their coats and personal items during work hours.
He placed his valuables in a locker before heading out.

senses.definition: a person sharing a room, apartment, or house with one or more people

examples.x: She met her roommate on the first day of college and they quickly became friends.
Living with a roommate can help reduce the cost of rent.
He and his roommate often cook dinner together in the shared kitchen.
They had some disagreements at first, but now they are getting along well as roommates.
Finding a compatible roommate is essential for a peaceful living environment.

senses.definition: the practice of using methods such as punishment, training, or guidance to enforce rules and improve behavior

examples.x: Effective discipline in the classroom helps maintain order and promotes a conducive learning environment.
The military emphasizes discipline as essential for unit cohesion and mission readiness.
A lack of discipline among team members can lead to inefficiency and missed deadlines in a workplace.
Personal discipline involves self-control and adherence to personal goals and values.

senses.definition: a university student who studies a particular subject as the main part of their course

examples.x: She is a biology major with a minor in chemistry.
He decided to change his major from business to psychology after his first year.
As an English major, she spends a lot of time reading and analyzing literature.

senses.definition: an official document that states one has successfully passed an exam or completed a course of study

examples.x: She received a certificate of completion after finishing the online training.
He framed his certificate and hung it on the wall in his office.
The certificate program is designed for working professionals looking to enhance their skills.
After passing the test, she was awarded a certificate in digital marketing.
You need a certificate in first aid to work as a lifeguard.

senses.definition: a person who holds a second university degree or an equivalent one

examples.x: She became a master in engineering after completing her graduate studies.
Many jobs require applicants to be a master in their field.
The university offers scholarships for those pursuing a master’s degree.
As a master in mathematics, she was offered a teaching position at the university.

senses.definition: to learn to perform or use a skill or ability thoroughly and completely

examples.x: The chef took the time to master the art of creating intricate desserts.
After years of practice, she finally mastered the skill of playing the guitar.
He aimed to master the language by immersing himself in daily conversations and literature.
The young artist worked hard to master different painting techniques.
The athlete mastered her routine, making it flawless in the competition.

senses.definition: a very high-level university degree given to a person who has conducted advanced research in a specific subject

examples.x: She earned her Doctor of Philosophy in neuroscience after six years of rigorous research.
The Doctor of Philosophy degree allowed her to specialize in her chosen field of study.

senses.definition: a graduate student who is studying at a university to get a more advanced degree

examples.x: She decided to continue her education as a postgraduate to earn a master's degree in psychology.
The university offers numerous programs for postgraduate students seeking advanced qualifications.
He spent several years as a postgraduate researching renewable energy solutions.
The postgraduate community at the university is vibrant, with students from all over the world.
As a postgraduate, she had access to additional resources and mentorship opportunities.

senses.definition: a person or organization that provides someone with financial supports for their education

examples.x: He found a sponsor who would cover his tuition fees and living expenses.
The foundation serves as a sponsor for many underprivileged students.
Without a sponsor, he would not have been able to afford his education abroad.
The artist's education was funded by a generous sponsor who believed in her potential.

senses.definition: a person who is being trained for a particular job or profession

examples.x: The company hired five new trainees this month.
As a trainee, she attended several training sessions.
The doctor supervised the medical trainee during surgery.
The trainee shadowed an experienced worker to learn the job.
He completed his trainee program and became a full-time employee.

senses.definition: to teach a single student or a few students, often outside a school setting

examples.x: She decided to tutor her classmates in chemistry to help them prepare for the upcoming exam.
The university offers a program where advanced students can tutor their peers in specific subjects.
He volunteered to tutor underprivileged children in mathematics during the weekends.

senses.definition: an original piece of writing on a particular subject that a candidate for a university degree presents based on their research

examples.x: She spent months conducting experiments and analyzing data for her thesis, which was an essential part of her university degree in chemistry.
Completing a thesis is a significant requirement for earning a university degree in most academic disciplines.
The doctoral candidate defended her thesis on quantum computing, presenting groundbreaking research that advances the field's understanding of quantum algorithms.

senses.definition: a sum of money given by an educational institution to someone with great ability in order to financially support their education

examples.x: She received a full scholarship to study medicine at a prestigious university.
His academic achievements earned him a scholarship that covered all his expenses.
The scholarship helped her pay for tuition, books, and housing while she completed her degree.
Applying for a scholarship requires a strong academic record and a compelling personal statement.
The university offers several scholarships to students from low-income backgrounds.

senses.definition: a class or course at a college or university in which a small group of students and a teacher discuss a specific subject

examples.x: The seminar focused on contemporary issues in international law.
She attended a seminar on environmental sustainability last week.
The students were required to present their research findings at the seminar.
He was excited to participate in the seminar because it allowed for more interactive discussions.
The professor led a seminar on the ethics of artificial intelligence.

senses.definition: the overall content, courses, and learning experiences designed by educational institutions to achieve specific educational goals and outcomes for students

examples.x: The school's curriculum includes core subjects like math, science, and language arts, as well as elective courses in arts and music.
The university revised its curriculum to incorporate more hands-on learning experiences and industry internships.
The curriculum for early childhood education emphasizes social-emotional development and play-based learning.
Teachers collaborate to develop a curriculum that meets state standards and prepares students for standardized tests.
The online platform provides access to resources and materials aligned with the curriculum for distance learning.

senses.definition: available or possible to choose but not required or forced

examples.x: The final project is optional, but many students choose to complete it for extra credit.
Attendance at the seminar is optional; students can attend if they are interested in the topic.
The elective course in photography is optional for students majoring in business administration.
The school offers optional extracurricular activities such as chess club and drama club after school.
The homework assignment is optional, but completing it will help reinforce the concepts learned in class.

senses.definition: to give a score to a student's performance

examples.x: The teacher spent the weekend grading her students' essays.
She was nervous about how the professor would grade her final project.
He is known to grade fairly but with high expectations.
After grading the tests, the instructor returned them to the students with feedback.
The professor explained the criteria she would use to grade the assignments.

senses.definition: the assignments, projects, and tasks done by students as part of their course of study

examples.x: She spent hours completing her coursework for the biology class, including lab reports and research papers.
The coursework for the computer science course involves programming assignments and algorithm analysis.
Students are expected to submit their coursework by the deadline to receive full credit.
The university requires students to complete a certain number of credit hours of coursework to graduate.
He struggled to balance his coursework with part-time work and extracurricular activities.

senses.definition: a book used for the study of a particular subject, especially in schools and colleges

examples.x: The textbook for the biology course is quite comprehensive.
She bought a new textbook for her economics class.
The professor recommended several textbooks for the course, including both classic and modern sources.
He used his textbook to prepare for the upcoming exams.
Textbooks can be expensive, but they are essential for studying.

senses.definition: a book that provides students with extra exercises

examples.x: The teacher assigned several pages from the workbook for homework.
She completed all the math problems in her workbook to prepare for the test.
The workbook offers additional practice questions for students struggling with the material.
Each chapter in the workbook is designed to reinforce the lessons from the main textbook.
The language course includes a workbook filled with grammar and vocabulary exercises.

senses.definition: (of a quiz, question, etc.) providing several different responses from which only one is correct

examples.x: The exam consisted mainly of multiple-choice questions.
She prefers multiple-choice tests because they allow her to eliminate incorrect answers.
The teacher created a multiple-choice quiz to assess the students' understanding of the topic.
Some multiple-choice questions were tricky because all the options seemed similar.
The online test included both multiple-choice and short-answer questions.

senses.definition: a course of instruction that is presented to an individual or a small number of students, typically focused on a specific subject or topic

examples.x: She attended a tutorial on essay writing to improve her academic writing skills.
The math tutorial covered advanced calculus concepts that were challenging for the students.
He scheduled a tutorial with his Spanish tutor to practice conversational skills and grammar.
The university offers tutorials in statistics to help students prepare for exams and understand complex data analysis.
The online tutorial included interactive exercises and quizzes to reinforce learning objectives.

senses.definition: a college or university building in which students reside

examples.x: He made many friends while living in the dormitory during his freshman year.
The dormitory is located near the library and the student center for easy access.

senses.definition: a room in a university, from which students can buy food and drink

examples.x: The buttery offers a variety of snacks and beverages for students between classes.
She stopped by the buttery to grab a coffee before heading to her lecture.
The buttery serves hot meals and sandwiches during lunch hours.
After studying all morning, they met at the buttery for a quick bite.
The buttery is a popular spot for students to relax and enjoy a cup of tea.
